RESOLUTION DECLARING THE RESULT OF THE CITY OF TUSCALOOSA MUNICIPAL
ELECTION HELD ON APRIL 14, 2026, EXCEPT THOSE VOTES CAST ON PROVISIONAL
BALLOTS TO ELECT THE DISTRICT 3 CITY COUNCILMEMBER
(OCA-25-1789)

WHEREAS, the special municipal election of the City of Tuscaloosa was duly held
on the 14" day of April 2026, in and for the City of Tuscaloosa, Alabama, a Municipal
Corporation, to elect the District 3 City Councilmember, for the remainder of the term of
office that began the third Monday in May 2025; and,

WHEREAS, at said election, the individuals, as indicated herein, were candidates
for the respective offices; and,

WHEREAS, pursuant to Ala. Code §11-46-55 commencing at 12:00 noon on
Tuesday after the election, the Municipal governing body shall proceed to open the
envelopes addressed to such governing body which have been delivered by the several
returning officers to the Municipal Clerk, canvass the returns and ascertain and determine
the number of votes received by each candidate and for and against each proposition
submitted at such election. If it appears that any candidate in such election has received
a majority of the votes cast for that office, the Municipal governing body shall declare said
candidate elected to such office, and a certificate of election shall be given to such person
by the Municipal governing body or a majority of them, which shall entitle the person so
certified to the possession of their respective offices immediately upon the expiration of
the terms of their predecessors as provided by law; and,

WHEREAS, pursuant to said law, the City Council of Tuscaloosa has met on this the
21° day of April 2026, at 12:00 p.m. and has canvassed the returns of said election with
the exception of those votes cast on Provisional Ballots as required by law.

NOW, THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED BY THE CITY COUNCIL OF TUSCALOOSA as
follows:

That the legal returns of said election pursuant to Ala. Code §11-46-55, except
those votes cast on Provisional Ballots, show that the named candidates received votes
as follows for the respective offices for which they were candidates, to wit:

RESOLUTION DECLARING THE RESULT OF THE CITY OF TUSCALOOSA MUNICIPAL
ELECTION HELD ON APRIL 14, 2026 TO INCLUDE THOSE VOTES CAST ON PROVISIONAL
BALLOTS, TO ELECT THE DISTRICT 3 CITY COUNCILMEMBER
(OCA-25-1789)

WHEREAS, the special municipal election of the City of Tuscaloosa was duly held
on the 14th day of April 2026, in and for the City of Tuscaloosa, Alabama, a Municipal
Corporation, to elect the District 3 City Councilmember, for the remainder of the term of
office that began the third Monday in May 2025; and,

WHEREAS, at said election, the individuals, as indicated herein, were candidates
for the respective offices; and,

WHEREAS, pursuant to Ala. Code §11-46-55 the governing body met at 12:00 p.m. on April
21, 2026 and opened the envelopes addressed to the governing body which had been
delivered by the several returning officers to the Municipal Clerk, canvassed the returns
and ascertained and determined the number of votes received by each candidate at such
election by resolution duly adopted at that time, except for those votes cast on
Provisional Ballots; and,

WHEREAS, pursuant Ala. Code §17-10-2(f}, commencing at noon, Tuesday, seven days
after the election, the municipal governing body, in the presence of watchers, tabulated
provisional ballots which had been certified by the Board of Registrars as cast by
registered and qualified voters of the voting places in which such ballots were cast; and,

WHEREAS, it is now necessary to amend that certain resolution adopted by the City
Council which declared the results of the City of Tuscaloosa Municipal Election to include
those provisional votes that were cast and subsequently certified by the Board of
Registrars as entitled to be counted.

NOW, THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED BY THE CITY COUNCIL OF TUSCALOOSA as follows:
1. That on April 21, 2026 at 12:00 p.m. the City Council of the City of Tuscaloosa did

convene and canvass the returns and ascertained and determined, by resolution duly
adopted, the number of votes received by each candidate as indicated in those envelops

addressed to said governing body which had been delivered by the several returning
officers to the Municipal Clerk, excluding provisional ballots; and,

2. Subsequent to the actions in the above paragraph, the City Council of the City of
Tuscaloosa has now reconvened in the presence of poll watchers and has tabulated
provisional ballots which have been certified by the Board of Registrars as cast by
registered and qualified voters of the voting places in which such ballots were cast.

3. That the resolution duly adopted by the City Council on April 21, 2026, at 12:00
p.m. canvassing the returns and ascertaining and determining the number of votes
received by each candidate, not including provisional ballots, be and the same is hereby
amended in its entirety to reflect, where appropriate, the canvass of said provisional
ballots.

4. That the legal returns of said election show that the named candidates received
votes as follows for the respective offices for which they were candidates, to wit:

April 28, 2026  
 
To:        Public Projects Committee  
  
From:   Brett Blackburn , PE 
              Office of the City Engineer  
  
RE:       2025.023.001 - Lincoln Park Drainage Improvements  
             Approval for Professional Services Agreement  
 
 
Request: 
The Office of the City Engineer (OCE) is requesting approval for  a Professional Services Agreement with  
Cabaniss Engineering , Inc.  for engineering services on the Lincoln Park Drainage Improvements  Project  
in the amount of  $109,560.00. 
 
The professional services include : 
• Preliminary and Final Design  • Easements and Right -of-Way Services  
• Design Survey and Testing  • Bid Phase Services  
• Regulatory Permitting Services  • Basic Engineering Services During Construction  
 
Project Description: 
Lincoln Park Neighborhood has experienced recurring flooding and poor drainage during rain events, primarily due to flat topography that limits natural runoff. This project aims to improve stormwater management and overall infrastructure in the area. The p roposed improvements include regrading and 
lowering the roadway to facilitate proper drainage from adjacent properties, installation of a new storm drainage piping network, and construction of curb and gutter systems. Additional enhancements will include n ew sidewalks, street lighting, and security cameras to improve pedestrian safety and 
neighborhood security. The project will also address aging underground utilities by replacing galvanized water lines identified during recent inspections as nearing the en d of their service life. Furthermore, 
sewer manholes will be replaced, and existing sewer lines will be relined.  
 Project Location: 
Lincoln Park Subdivision, 42nd Ave (District 1) . 
 
Funding : 
Account # 22240330- 2650 9 
4

April 28, 2026

From:   Brett Blackburn , PE 
              Office of the City Engineer  
  
RE:       2023.006.001 - 10th Ave at 19th St. Drainage

Request: 
The Office of the City Engineer (OCE) is requesting approval for  a Professional Services Agreement with  
Volkert, Inc.  for engineering services on the 10th Ave at 19th St. Drainage  Project  in the amount of  
$512,242.00. 
 
The professional services include : 
• Preliminary and Final Design  • Easements and Right -of-Way Services  
• Design Survey and Testing  • Bid Phase Services  
• Regulatory Permitting Services  • Basic Engineering Services During Construction  
 
Project Description: 
This project includes improving storm drainage network from the intersection of 10th Avenue and 19th Street downstream (southwest) to the storm drainage ditch near Bonita Terrace. This area has experienced chronic and worsening flooding during rain events,  impacting residential properties, local 
infrastructure, and public safety. A final drainage study was conducted which identified significant deficiencies in the existing storm sewer system, including undersized and deteriorated pipes, poor connectivity, a nd capacity shortfalls. The proposed improvements include the design of a new storm 
sewer outfall system capable of conveying a 25 -year storm event, featuring a box culvert and reinforced 
concrete piping along a revised alignment from Hargrove Road north t o 17th Street.  
 Project Location: 
Intersection of 10th Ave & 19th St (District 2). 
 
Funding : 
Account # 22240330- 2650 3
